grouped bars per benchmark · x = size or swept parameter · bar heights normalised within each size (relative comparison, not absolute throughput) · FArray = bold emerald, competitors muted · band behind each size + card frame tinted by verdict (green win · gray tie · red loss) vs the best competitor · hover a size to compare numbers · 45 non-ops/s skipped

How to read this. For every benchmark at every size, the fastest structure there scores 1.00; every other scores how many times slower it ran in that cell. Each number below is the geometric mean of those per-cell ratios across the group (geometric so a handful of extreme ops do not dominate). Lower is better — 1.00 = fastest everywhere, 3.0 = typically 3× off the best. Columns are the benchmark groups; TOTAL is overall.
